name: Hrolf Kraki
description: Hrolf Kraki, also known as Hrólfr Kraki or Rolf Krake, was a legendary Danish king who ruled from Lejre. He is a prominent figure in Norse sagas and poems, renowned for his wisdom, generosity, and bravery. Hrolf was the son of Helgi (Halga) and Yrsa, who was later revealed to be Helgi's own daughter, creating a tragic and incestuous lineage. He succeeded his uncle Hroar as king and gathered a band of loyal and exceptionally skilled warriors around him, known as the champions of Hrolf Kraki. These warriors included figures like Bodvar Bjarki, Hjalti, and Vogg. Hrolf's court at Lejre was famous for its hospitality and wealth, attracting heroes and adventurers from far and wide. His reign was marked by numerous battles and challenges, including conflicts with the Swedish king Adils (Eadgils), a powerful and often treacherous ruler. Hrolf's ultimate downfall came when he was betrayed and killed by his brother-in-law, Hjort, who coveted the throne. Despite his tragic end, Hrolf Kraki remains a symbol of kingship, courage, and loyalty in Norse tradition, embodying the ideals of a heroic leader and a generous patron. His legend served as an inspiration for later Scandinavian rulers and continues to resonate in modern interpretations of Norse mythology.
subtitle: The Generous King of Lejre
achievements: Gathered a band of unparalleled warriors
Maintained a renowned court at Lejre
Defeated the Swedish king Adils in battle (indirectly)
Embodied the ideal of a generous king
Became a symbol of loyalty and courage

keyMyths.title: The Incestuous Lineage
keyMyths.description: Hrolf Kraki was born of an incestuous relationship between Helgi and his daughter Yrsa. This tragic origin casts a shadow over his life and reign, highlighting the themes of fate and destiny in Norse mythology. This event is a major plot point in the Saga of Hrolf Kraki.

keyMyths.title: The Battle on the Ice
keyMyths.description: Hrolf Kraki and his warriors confronted the Swedish king Adils on a frozen lake. Adils, known for his cunning, attempted to trick Hrolf, but Hrolf's warriors proved their strength and loyalty, ultimately escaping with treasure. This shows the cunning of Adils and the strength of Hrolf's warriors.

keyMyths.title: The Treachery of Hjort
keyMyths.description: Hrolf Kraki was betrayed and killed by his brother-in-law Hjort, who desired the throne. This betrayal highlights the themes of ambition and the fragility of loyalty, ultimately leading to the king's downfall. This event marked the end of Hrolf Kraki's reign.

sources.author: Unknown
